spec_match.rationale: Probes seven properties × two access styles (getter + method), surfaces match / differ / gone counts — exactly the deprecation-runway dashboard the chromestatus entry wants.

interactive.rationale: Locale input + Enter + run button + pill-style quick-locales (10 controls). Re-renders the whole grid live.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises 'counts matches, diffs, and properties already removed — a one-shot deprecation runway audit.' Page delivers the counter row at the top.

edge_cases.rationale: Five-way state machine (match / diff / gone / method-missing / neither-shipped) covers the cells well. But the same `timeFormats` mismatch as the migration-helper page is visible here too — props list omits timeFormats, which the codemod sibling includes. Also: JSON.stringify equality is order-sensitive.

references.rationale: See-also includes https://chromestatus.com/feature/5148228059398144 and the TC39 proposal.

design_system.rationale: Variables-only; gone/match/diff cells use accent colours; counters use --text-charcoal.

openQuestions.title: Align the props list with the codemod sibling
openQuestions.detail: Migration-helper includes `timeFormats` in its rewrite list; this matrix doesn't probe it. Either add the row here or remove it from the codemod — currently the two pages disagree about the API surface.

openQuestions.title: Capture deprecation warnings live
openQuestions.detail: Register a ReportingObserver for type 'deprecation' before each cell's getter access; surface 'console warned: …' in the cell when it fires. That's the actual signal Chrome 143 provides — and counting how many warnings the page generated is a real-world deprecation audit.

openQuestions.title: Replace JSON.stringify equality with order-insensitive compare
openQuestions.detail: Objects returned by getCalendars() vs .calendars are equal in content but their array order isn't guaranteed across V8 versions. A small Set-based equality check would stop false-positive 'differ' verdicts when the values are functionally identical.

openQuestions.title: Add `getTextInfo().direction` as its own column
openQuestions.detail: The matrix shows the whole textInfo object as one cell — but the practically-most-used field is direction. Surfacing direction explicitly (with a small RTL/LTR badge) would make the dashboard more skimmable.

summary: Strongest dashboard concept in this feature — five-way state machine with totals is exactly the right shape for a deprecation audit. Two fixes: align the props list with the codemod sibling (both include / both omit timeFormats), and surface ReportingObserver deprecation warnings so the page can also count Chrome-side noise.
